summary:
Glycopyrrolate has a reduced ability to enter the brain and crosses the placenta minimally because of its chemical composition; this distinguishes it from other similar drugs with greater potential neurologic effects during pregnancy. Summary sentence count: 2
The summary highlights why glycopyrrolate stands apart as an option meeting both requirements by emphasizing its unique lack of significant passage across critical biological membranes without unnecessary detail. To ensure brevity strictly adheres:

Key point: Glycopyrrolate limits entry into the brain and placentation better than alternatives making it suitable when such risks are concerning. This concise statement captures essential aspects efficiently addressing the core issue posed initially within two succinct phrases aligned closely around required focus areas.
